summary: Scorching Vegas show with epic Bird Song + Santana and tight Deal>Drums
blurb: The first set opens strong with a pristine Jack Straw and a soaring Bird Song featuring guest Carlos Santana. The second set delivers a hot Deal → Drums → Space stretch and powerful jams on Crazy Fingers, Truckin' and The Other One. Phil’s bass is punchy throughout while Bill and Mickey keep the rhythm section on fire.
review:
April 28, 1991 at the Sam Boyd Silver Bowl captured a high‑octane Dead night that still feels fresh. A guest appearance by Carlos Santana turns the already soaring Bird Song into a transcendent duet, and the Vegas‑themed Deal → Drums transition (followed by an expansive Space) rides the slot‑machine energy of the venue perfectly. The first set rolls from Jack Straw into Althea and then bursts through Bird Song, while the second set builds momentum with Crazy Fingers, Truckin', and a marathon The Other One that keeps the crowd in a hypnotic groove. Phil Lesh’s bass is described as “blazing” and “growling,” anchoring Bill Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart who are “on fire” throughout, especially during the Deal → Drums highlight. Keyboard duties from Bruce Hornsby and Vince Welnick add supportive textures, though some reviewers note an over‑saturated synth on Saint Of Circumstance. A few songs—Saint Of Circumstance, Foolish Heart and the later Deal—lose a bit of steam, but overall the performance is tight, energetic, and uniquely Vegas‑flavored.

For listeners seeking the clearest window onto this night, the Miller soundboard (gd1991-04-28.sbd.miller.92278.sbeok.flac16) offers crisp, well‑crafted audio with no dropouts, capturing both the subtle nuances of the keyboards and the full force of the drums. Whether you’re chasing the Santana‑infused Bird Song or the fiery Deal → Drums → Space sequence, this show delivers a memorable snapshot of the Dead in their early‑’90s prime.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across all recordings: multiple reviewers call the show 'scorching', 'epic Bird Song with Santana', and praise the Deal>Drums > Space sequence. No consistent negative criticism of performance; only isolated comments about weaker vocals or a few slower songs, so a robust +1.6 adjustment is appropriate.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture—crisp, outstanding clarity with no dropouts, preserving the full energy of the performance.

key_highlights: Transcendent Bird Song featuring guest Carlos Santana
Deal → Drums → Space stretch that perfectly fits the Vegas vibe
Tight rhythm section—Bill and Mickey on fire throughout
Phil Lesh’s blazing, punchy bass driving the jam momentum

band_performance.Jerry: Leads the night with a soaring Bird Song duet with Santana and strong lead work; vocals noted as a bit weaker on some recordings but overall energetic.
band_performance.Phil: Blazing, punchy bass that fuels the show’s energy; described as relentless and growling throughout.
band_performance.Bob: Big grin early in the set; solid rhythm guitar with captivating improvisational figures during jams.
band_performance.Vince: Mixed feedback—praised for piano on Jack Straw and supportive touches on Bird Song, but over‑saturated synth on Saint Of Circumstance detracts slightly; overall keyboards support the energy.
band_performance.Mickey: On fire throughout, especially highlighted in the Deal → Drums transition.
band_performance.Billy: On fire throughout, driving the rhythmic power; drums sometimes sound high‑level in recordings but remain powerful.
